insight: Iran's performance in the 2026 FIFA World Cup has shown a notable trend when it comes to hydration breaks, with the team gaining momentum in both of their breaks so far, resulting in a mean increase of 0.200 points per minute. This suggests that the breaks have given Iran a chance to regroup and come back stronger, potentially catching their opponents off guard. Notably, in the instances where Iran was not pressing before the break, they were able to maintain their momentum after the break, with an average increase of 0.200 points per minute, indicating that the team is able to capitalize on the brief pause in play to refocus and push forward. Overall, the hydration breaks seem to be having a positive impact on Iran's gameplay, allowing them to shift the momentum in their favor and potentially change the course of the match.
